rag_rules: AGREE annex blocks must be placed in component type scopes for assumptions/guarantees involving ports, and in implementation scopes for bindings.
Component names, ports, and connections are defined in the AADL model and must be referenced exactly as specified.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL scoping rules when inserting AGREE contracts.
Only reference AADL identifiers that are explicitly declared in the model.
Avoid encoding AADL properties like Period, Deadline, Priority as AGREE variables unless typed ports or constants are available.
Do not encode event ports as numeric or Boolean values unless an explicit data value is provided.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Implementation-level bindings must be placed in implementation scopes after subcomponents and connections.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL properties such as Period or Deadline as AGREE variables without explicit typed ports.
Do not treat event ports as numeric or Boolean values without model-provided data.
